back_translation: And David had crossed a little beyond the summit, and behold, Ziba the young man of Mephibosheth was to meet him, with a pair of saddled donkeys, and upon them two hundred loaves of bread and a hundred clusters of raisins and a hundred summer fruits and a skin of wine.

And the king said to Ziba, 'What are these to you?' And Ziba said, 'The donkeys are for the king's household to ride, and the bread and the summer fruit for the young men to eat, and the wine for the exhausted in the wilderness to drink.'

And the king said, 'And where is your master's grandson?' And Ziba said to the king, 'Behold, he is staying in Jerusalem, for he said, Today the house of Israel will restore to me the kingdom of my father.'
And the king said to Ziba, 'Behold, to you is everything that belongs to Mephibosheth.' And Ziba said, 'I bow down; let me find favor in your eyes, my lord the king.'
And King David came as far as Bahurim, and behold, from there a man was coming out from the family of the house of Saul, and his name was Shimei son of Gera; coming out, coming out, and cursing.
And he stoned David and all the servants of King David; and all the people and all the mighty men were on his right and on his left.
And thus said Shimei in his cursing, 'Get out, get out, you man of bloodshed and man of Belial!

The LORD has returned upon you all the blood of the house of Saul, in whose place you reigned, and the LORD has given the kingdom into the hand of Absalom your son; and behold, you are in your evil, for a man of bloodshed are you.'
And Abishai son of Zeruiah said to the king, 'Why should this dead dog curse my lord the king? Let me cross over please and remove his head.'
And the king said, 'What to me and to you, sons of Zeruiah? Thus he curses, because the LORD has said to him, Curse David; and who shall say, Why have you done so?'

And David said to Abishai and to all his servants, 'Behold, my son who came forth from my body seeks my life; how much more then this Benjamite. Let him alone and let him curse, for the LORD has said to him.
Perhaps the LORD will look on my affliction, and the LORD will repay me with good in place of his cursing this day.'
And David and his men went on the way, and Shimei was walking on the side of the hill opposite him, going and cursing, and throwing stones opposite him, and flinging dust.
And the king and all the people who were with him came exhausted, and he refreshed himself there.
And Absalom and all the people, men of Israel, came to Jerusalem, and Ahithophel with him.
And it happened, when Hushai the Archite, friend of David, came to Absalom, that Hushai said to Absalom, 'Long live the king! Long live the king!'
And Absalom said to Hushai, 'Is this your steadfast love for your friend? Why did you not go with your friend?'
And Hushai said to Absalom, 'No, but the one whom the LORD has chosen, and this people, and all the men of Israel — his I will be, and with him I will dwell.
And secondly, whom should I serve? Is it not before his son? As I served before your father, so I will be before you.'
And Absalom said to Ahithophel, 'Give your counsel — what shall we do?'

And Ahithophel said to Absalom, 'Go in to the concubines of your father whom he left to keep the house; and all Israel will hear that you have become a stench to your father, and the hands of all who are with you will be strengthened.'
And they pitched for Absalom the tent on the roof, and Absalom went in to the concubines of his father in the sight of all Israel.
And the counsel of Ahithophel which he counseled in those days was as if one inquired by the word of God; so was all the counsel of Ahithophel both to David and to Absalom.
